One Apartments — Centralised Operations System
Case Study · Monday.com · Short-Term Rental

One Apartments — Centralised Operations Management System

Monday.comWorkflow AutomationCRMProperty Management
A centralised operations system built on Monday.com for One Apartments — a short-term rental company operating across Bangkok, Edinburgh, London, Manchester, and Phuket. The system consolidates maintenance tracking, guest experience management, and procurement into a single structured platform, replacing fragmented WhatsApp and email communication with accountable, trackable workflows.
The Problem

One Apartments managed five city locations with no centralised system. Maintenance requests arrived via WhatsApp, email, and Airbnb. Guest complaints were untracked. Inventory management was manual. There was no visibility into task ownership, resolution timelines, or cross-property performance.

The Solution

A Monday.com workspace centralising Maintenance, Guest Experience, and Procurement into structured boards with defined workflows, task ownership, status tracking, and automated notifications. All incoming requests are funnelled into a single source of truth.

System Breakdown
🔧Maintenance
  • Unique IDs (MAINT-001) per issue
  • Kanban, Calendar, Gantt, Table views
  • Priority, assigned staff, cost tracking
  • Multi-channel intake: WhatsApp, Email, Airbnb
🏨Guest Experience
  • Guest IDs (GX-001) linked to properties
  • Issue types: Aesthetic, Cleanliness, Inventory, Complaint
  • Source tracking: Guest Review, Staff, Manager
  • Status pipeline: Reported → In Progress → Fixed
📦Procurement
  • Item tracking with quantity, cost, supplier
  • Linked to property and department
  • Status: Requested → Ordered → Delivered
  • Connected to Maintenance for triggered restocking
Key Features
  • Automated status notifications on issue updates and task assignments
  • Multi-channel intake: WhatsApp, Email, Airbnb, Booking.com
  • Custom dashboard showing open issues, in-progress tasks, and costs per property
  • Cross-board linking between Maintenance, Guest Experience, and Procurement
  • Kanban, Calendar, Gantt, and Table views for different management roles
  • Issues FORM for structured intake directly into the Monday.com board
Data Implementation

25 real maintenance issues were structured and loaded into the system — each assigned a unique ID, property, priority level, responsible staff member, cost estimate, and resolution status — demonstrating the system under real operational conditions.

KAY Property — Monday.com System Build
Case Study · Monday.com · Buyer's Advocacy

From Scattered Notes to a Scalable System — KAY Property

Monday.comCRM ArchitectureWorkflow AutomationReal Estate
A full Monday.com workspace built for KAY Property — an Australian buyer's advocacy firm managing both local and interstate property deals. The system replaced a disorganised, manual setup with structured client boards, internal process tracking, and an insights dashboard — giving the team complete visibility from first contact to settlement.
Hero Result
4
Boards Built
6.5M
Portfolio Tracked
100%
Manual Work Eliminated
Client Context

KAY Property is a buyer's advocacy business operating across local and interstate Australian markets. The team manages multiple active clients simultaneously — each with their own property search, due diligence, negotiation, and settlement process. Without a structured system, managing this across multiple people and markets was unsustainable.

The Problem
📋
No Standard WorkflowEvery client was managed differently. No consistent process from onboarding to settlement.
👁️
Poor Client VisibilityNo central view of where each client was in their journey or what action was needed.
🔄
Excessive Manual TrackingProperty data, documents, and client updates were tracked manually — fragile and time-consuming.
📊
No Market Insights StructureNo organised system for rental yields, growth data, or vacancy rates across target markets.
The Solution — 3 Board Structure
🏠Client Boards
  • Property observations per client with status (Positive, In Review, Not Appropriate)
  • Campaign type, price range, auction dates, real estate links
  • Rental yield data per property
  • Intake form for structured property submissions
⚙️Internal Process Boards
  • Full client checklist: Onboarding, Engagement Sign, Invoice, Offer Accepted
  • Status columns with Yes/No/In Progress tracking per client
  • Client email, property address, and key dates in one view
  • Separate investment tracking board with purchased price and yield achieved
📊Insights Dashboard
  • Live chart: property observations by status (Positive, In Review, Not Appropriate)
  • Campaign type breakdown (Auction vs Private Treaty)
  • Total purchased price widget — 6.5M portfolio tracked
  • Geographic map view of all properties across Australia
Key Features
  • Automated reminders and status update notifications across all boards
  • Structured column system: dates, price ranges, links, checkboxes, status labels
  • Clean naming convention across all boards for consistent team use
  • Intake form for structured property observation submissions
  • Cross-linked client data between Client Facing and Internal Process boards
  • Dashboard pulling live data from all 4 connected boards
Results
⚡
Faster Team OperationsEvery client, property, and task has a defined home. The team spends less time searching and more time closing.
🎯
Clear Client ExperienceClients interact through a structured form. Nothing gets lost. Every submission is tracked automatically.
📉
Manual Work EliminatedStatus tracking, checklist updates, and reporting previously done manually are now structured and automated.
📈
Scalable FoundationThe system is built to grow — new clients, new boards, or new cities can be added without rebuilding from scratch.
Tools Used
Monday.comMonday AutomationsMonday FormsMonday DashboardsMonday ChartsMonday Maps

About Me

Who is Christopher Onu?

I'm Christopher Ufedojo Onu, a CRM & AI Automation Specialist. I help businesses replace fragmented, manual processes with intelligent automated systems that run 24/7.

My work spans CRM platform architecture, AI voice agent deployment, workflow automation, and multi-platform integrations — designed to cut operational costs and increase revenue.

Every system I build solves a specific business pain point — whether it's a scattered operations workflow, an untracked client pipeline, or a manual booking process.
